name: Buckingham Palace
title: Crown and country
note: Pictures for the big-picture opening: the palace and its guards, and the Parliament where the real power sits.

items.caption: Buckingham Palace, the London home of the monarch. This is the face of the Crown, the pageantry, though the real power sits a mile away at Westminster.
items.kidcaption: Buckingham Palace, where the King stays in London. When the flag is up, he's home!

items.caption: A guardsman in his bearskin. The ceremony and tradition around the monarchy is dazzling, and deliberately so, a living piece of theatre a thousand years old.
items.kidcaption: A palace guard in his tall furry hat. They must stand incredibly still, no matter how many tourists make funny faces!

items.caption: The Houses of Parliament at Westminster, where power actually lives. Britain is ruled by elected politicians here, while the king reigns but does not rule.
items.kidcaption: The Houses of Parliament, with Big Ben. This is where the people who really run the country argue and make the laws.
